mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signal strengthening aggregate demand and higher capacity utilization, which compresses the unemployment gap and raises the natural rate of interest, driving the 10-year Treasury yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS increases month-over-month but the 10-year yield falls more than 60% of the time over the subsequent 20 trading days, or if the rank IC is below 0.02 over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A decisive drop in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.0% triggers expectations of tighter labor markets and eventual monetary tightening, causing the 10-year yield to rise over the following 60 trading days as the market prices in higher policy rates.
failure_condition: After the trigger activates, the 10-year yield fails to rise (or falls) in more than 50% of instances within the next 60 trading days, or the average yield change is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production (INDPRO) is rising and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains at extremely low levels, the joint signal indicates robust real activity without immediate policy tightening, pushing the 10-year yield upward over a 60-day horizon as growth expectations dominate.
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with accommodative monetary policy
failure_condition: If both INDPRO increases and FEDFUNDS is below 1% but the 10-year yield declines over the next 60 trading days in more than 40% of such episodes, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a zero lower bound regime (FEDFUNDS < 1%), a rise in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) signals that the bound is constraining real rates, leading to higher expected inflation and a subsequent rise in the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days as break-even rates adjust.
regime_hint: zero lower bound regime with accommodative policy
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS < 1% and the 10-year yield does not rise in the subsequent 60 trading days in at least 60% of observations, this regime-conditional hypothesis is falsified.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in real retail and food services sales (RSAFS) reflect strengthening consumer spending, which drives GDP growth expectations and raises the natural interest rate, pushing the 10-year yield higher over the next 5 trading days as markets quickly incorporate the demand signal.
failure_condition: If RSAFS increases month-over-month but the 10-year yield declines over the following 5 trading days in more than 50% of cases, this always-on signal is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above 1.0% marks the start of a normalization cycle, signaling to markets that the central bank is confident in the economic recovery; this forward guidance effect lifts the 10-year yield over the next 60 trading days as term premiums reprice.
failure_condition: If after the trigger the 10-year yield does not rise on average over the next 60 trading days (mean change ≤ 0), or if the yield falls in more than 60% of triggered instances, this threshold hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) are increasing and the unemployment rate (UNRATE) is declining simultaneously, the joint signal points to a self-reinforcing recovery in housing and labor markets, which amplifies growth expectations and pushes the 10-year yield higher over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST rises month-over-month and UNRATE declines, but the 10-year yield declines in the following 20 trading days more than 40% of the time, the interaction hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market regime where unemployment is below 5%, continued payroll growth (PAYEMS) signals wage inflation pressures and imminent capacity constraints, causing market participants to bid up the 10-year yield in anticipation of tighter monetary policy over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5%, PAYEMS increases month-over-month, but the 10-year yield fails to rise over the next 5 trading days in more than 50% of instances; or if the average yield change is not positive and statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).
